On the night of his murder, Becker entered the alley at 11:52 p.m., just seconds before three men ran in behind him.
Surveillance video from the scene showed three men running down the alley shortly after Becker entered.
Within 20 seconds, the sound of gunfire echoed through the neighborhood.

The Ramsey County medical examiner found Becker had been shot six times and his death was a homicide. The vehicle the suspects fled in was identified as a 2017 Toyota Camry with a damaged passenger side headlight. It had been reported stolen from Brooklyn Center the previous day. Officers recovered a handgun near the spot where Kinnerd hid. Ballistics testing confirmed that this weapon matched the shell casings found next to Becker's body.
He got freaked out when he saw you. I don't know if you noticed that.
Police arrested Kinnerd on January 3rd at a gas station in North Minneapolis. According to the criminal complaint, when Kinnerd spotted officers, his eyes widened and he ducked down behind a shelf.

Inside his apartment, investigators found crucial pieces of evidence, including clothing that matched what Kinnerd had worn on the night of the murder. A photo from his phone, dated just days before the shooting, showed him wearing a yellow hoodie while holding a handgun.

The evidence against Kinnerd was overwhelming. Surveillance footage captured him and two others leaving an apartment on the night of the shooting, wearing the same clothes as those seen in the video from the crime scene. The stolen Camry was also linked back to the apartment building where Kinnerd was staying.

In court, Becker's family shared their grief. His mother Tara spoke about the kind, loyal person Alex was and how his murder left a hole in their lives. Kinnerd, however, remained silent during his sentencing.
If you knew him, you loved him. I don't he was just really special. The world is missing out without him here.

In February 2024, Kinnerd pleaded guilty to second-degree murder and was sentenced to 363 months, or roughly 30 years. He's required to serve about 20 years behind bars, with the remaining time on supervised release. Detwan Allen, the second man involved, was found guilty in December 2023 and sentenced to more than 30 years in prison. Judge Paul Yang described the murder as more than a robbery, saying, "You chased down your prey and shot him like target practice."

So from the outside, it look like the robbery, but when you really look closer at the evidence, it's like you you you guys are hunting. You chase down your prey, you surround him and you shot the prey as your target practice. And this is a human being we talk about, Mr. Becker.

A third defendant, Sean Lamar Travis, faced trial as well, but he was acquitted after a bench trial in December 2023. The judge ruled there was not enough evidence to convict him of Becker's murder. Kinnerd's criminal history, which includes prior convictions for aggravated robbery, added weight to the judge's decision. He will remain incarcerated until at least the 2040s, leaving Alex's family to cope with his loss.
